{"key_event":"Full-scale Sino-Japanese War and occupation","reasoning":"Nanjing Decade reforms, currency centralization, and coercive party-state building raised Nationalist capacity in core areas, but Japanese conquest after 1937, loss of revenue-rich regions, warlord bargains, and relocation to Chongqing left overall Chinese state power still weak-to-moderate. Autonomous society was damaged by KMT repression, rural distress, occupation violence, refugee disruption, and disasters such as the Yellow River flood, only partly offset by nationalist resistance and Communist-led rural mobilization.","society_change":-1.0,"society_power":4.8,"state_change":-0.3,"state_power":4.4}
